mfa.note: 95% of pyruvate flux to acetate; 0.5%/4.1% to lactate/ethanol at low PH2

growth_records.cite: Willquist et al. Reassessment of hydrogen tolerance in Caldicellulosiruptor saccharolyticus. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-111. [PMC3339340]
growth_records.snippet: Fermentation data in continuous cultivations of C.saccharolyticus on glucose (5g/L) at steady states... D 0.05 (100 mL/min N2): q_glucose 2.90+/-0.09, q_H2 10.1+/-0.4; yields H2 3.48, acetate 1.61, lactate 0.01, ethanol 0.07 mol/mol; biomas
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, mu=D=0.05/h. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.05 = 13.86 h. q_glucose(2.90) and q_H2(10.1) reported directly (Table 1). Secretion rates computed from reported molar product yields x q_glucose: acetate 1.61*2.90=4.67; lactate 0.01*2.90=0.029; ethanol 0.07*2.90=0.203 mmol/gDW/h. Full DSM640 salt/vitamin composition not given in text (cited to ref 15); only glucose 5 g/L and cysteine-HCl 0.25 g/L stated. Case I = with N2 sparging (low PH2 ~6.3 kPa).

mfa.note: 86% of pyruvate flux directed to acetate at D=0.15 vs 95% at D=0.05

growth_records.cite: Willquist et al. Reassessment of hydrogen tolerance in Caldicellulosiruptor saccharolyticus. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-111. [PMC3339340]
growth_records.snippet: D 0.15 (100 mL/min N2): biomass 0.61 g/L, residual glucose 3 mM, q_glucose 6.2+/-0.4, q_pyruvate 9.55, q_H2 18.0+/-0.0; yields H2 2.9, acetate 1.32, lactate 0.01, ethanol 0.20 mol/mol; biomass 24.1 g/mol
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, mu=D=0.15/h. Doubling time = ln2/0.15 = 4.62 h. q_glucose(6.2), q_pyruvate(9.55) and q_H2(18.0) reported (Table 1). Secretion computed from molar yields x q_glucose: acetate 1.32*6.2=8.18; lactate 0.01*6.2=0.062; ethanol 0.20*6.2=1.24 mmol/gDW/h. Residual glucose higher (3 mM) at this D. Case I (N2-sparged, low PH2).

up.method: chemostat steady-state q_glucose (reported Table 1); note q_glucose not reduced despite high PH2

mfa.note: at high PH2 (67 kPa) 38% and 16% of pyruvate flux redirected to lactate and ethanol respectively

growth_records.cite: Willquist et al. Reassessment of hydrogen tolerance in Caldicellulosiruptor saccharolyticus. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-111. [PMC3339340]
growth_records.snippet: D 0.05 no stripping: biomass 0.42 g/L, q_glucose 3.2+/-0.4, q_pyruvate 5.8+/-0.7, q_H2 5.9+/-0.6; yields H2 1.82, acetate 0.83, lactate 0.67, ethanol 0.28 mol/mol; biomass 16 g/mol. PH2 reached 67 kPa without washout at D=0.05
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, mu=D=0.05/h, no gas sparging (elevated PH2 ~67 kPa). Doubling time = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h. q_glucose(3.2), q_pyruvate(5.8), q_H2(5.9) reported (Table 1). Secretion computed from molar yields x q_glucose: acetate 0.83*3.2=2.66; lactate 0.67*3.2=2.14; ethanol 0.28*3.2=0.896 mmol/gDW/h. High PH2 redirects 38%/16% of pyruvate flux to lactate/ethanol. Culture washed out at higher D=0.15 without sparging (no steady state).

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
